title: Oversized optional call graph must not block core impact context
goal: Keep coherent path, symbol, test, contract and entrypoint impact context usable when python_call_graph_json is missing, oversized or otherwise untrusted, while degrading only call-graph-derived relations with explicit provenance gaps and preserving fail-closed behavior for required core artifacts.

acceptance.assertion: python_call_graph_json is not part of required core coherence; missing, oversized, blocked, stale or mismatched call-graph evidence cannot block otherwise coherent architecture, symbol and entrypoint sources.
Edit context reports unavailable or untrusted call-graph coverage explicitly and emits no trusted direct caller/callee relation from that source.
Target symbols, related tests and other core impact evidence remain available when the optional call graph degrades, subject to their own source freshness and budgets.
Tampering, mismatch or unavailability of required core artifacts remains blocking and is covered by negative tests.
The Weltgewebe vertical-pilot blocker artifact_too_large no longer collapses the full agent impact context; the pilot is rerun before any promotion decision.
Focused tests and full RepoGround test suite are green; exact head and diff SHA-256 are reviewed before merge.

metadata.source_finding: Weltgewebe vertical pilot observed python_call_graph_json at 22127878 bytes above the 16777216-byte read-only adapter bound; the optional source currently blocks artifact coherence and removes related_tests and target_symbols from composed change context.
metadata.scope_reason: The pilot can only satisfy its delivery-chain acceptance after optional call-graph failure is isolated from required core impact sources; T003 itself has read-only RepoGround scope.
metadata.outcome: No additional RepoGround implementation was required at closeout. The later canonical REPOGROUND-AGENT-UTILITY-V1-T007 work already implemented and verified the same large optional-call-graph degradation boundary through RepoGround PRs #1071 and #1073. Current RepoGround main retains that behavior, focused regression tests pass, exact-head CI is green, and the fresh Weltgewebe four-case pilot no longer reports artifact_too_large or impact_context_blocked. RPU-V1-T034 is therefore verified as satisfied-by-existing-canonical-implementation rather than by a redundant new patch.

validated_acceptance.core-coherence: PASS: On RepoGround main dea8e61ace8bc45a32ec32bff3f79eccaa9c9f19, merger/repoground/core/agent_impact_context.py defines _CORE_SOURCES as architecture_graph_json, python_symbol_index_json and entrypoints_json only. python_call_graph_json is not a required coherence source and cannot block otherwise coherent core sources.
validated_acceptance.explicit-degradation: PASS: _call_graph_context emits call_graph_source_unavailable for missing call-graph evidence and call_graph_source_untrusted for non-coherent evidence, with python_call_graph_json freshness provenance, while returning no trusted direct caller/callee relations from that source.
validated_acceptance.core-impact-survives: PASS: RepoGroundAgentImpactAdapter first builds target-symbol/core impact context without call relations, then projects only relevant call records through the bounded call-graph index. T007's fresh four-case Weltgewebe pilot reports 4/4 quality_pass with no impact_context_blocked; the generic >16 MiB artifact limit no longer collapses core impact context.
validated_acceptance.fail-closed-required: PASS: Required core artifacts remain governed by _CORE_SOURCES and blocking source statuses. Focused current-main tests cover missing, mismatched and tampered optional call graphs plus tamper, manifest, candidate-count and projection-byte fail-closed paths.
validated_acceptance.pilot-regression: PASS: REPOGROUND-AGENT-UTILITY-V1-T007 records a fresh four-case Weltgewebe pilot on bundle heimgewebe__weltgewebe__main-max-260721-1911 with quality_pass 4/4, artifact_too_large 0 and impact_context_blocked 0. Its later T003 observation on bundle heimgewebe__weltgewebe__main-max-260721-2115 again reports artifact_too_large 0 and promotion gold cases 3/3; T003 remains blocked only by separate contract/runtime/recovery evidence gaps.
validated_acceptance.delivery: PASS: The implementing canonical changes were reviewed and merged through RepoGround PR #1071 and #1073 with recorded exact base/head diff bindings in REPOGROUND-AGENT-UTILITY-V1-T007. On current RepoGround main dea8e61ace8bc45a32ec32bff3f79eccaa9c9f19, focused adapter/context/call-graph-index tests pass 51/51 and exact-head GitHub workflows test-suite, lint, CodeQL, contracts-validate, ai-context guard and task-index are successful.
